member.summary: Resolves local resource path of scenario scripts from their file's URI.

member.summary: Relative (to the project root) location of a directory where all the scenario script files are stored; starts with '/' but doesn't end with '/'.
member.remarks: Can be resolved with .

member.name: M:Naninovel.ScriptPathResolver.Resolve(System.String)
member.summary: Resolves local resource path of a scenario script with specified file location. Results are memoized on consequent requests with same file and root URIs.
member.remarks: When specified path doesn't contain , will keep all the parent directories after the first separator; in case path doesn't contain separators, returns it as-is, but w/o the .nani extension.

member.name: T:Naninovel.Metadata.Branch
member.summary: Describes script playback flow branching caused by a execution.

member.name: P:Naninovel.Metadata.Branch.Traits
member.summary: Traits of the branching; may have multiple flags (bitmask).

member.name: P:Naninovel.Metadata.Branch.SwitchRoots
member.summary: Indicates that the command is a part of a switch block, which starts at the command with one of the specified IDs. For example, @else command has 'if' and 'unless' as switch roots, indicating that the playback, once returned from either nested @if, @unless or other @else blocks will skip the consequent @else blocks.

member.name: P:Naninovel.Metadata.Branch.Endpoint
member.summary: When the branch is , specifies a well-known endpoint or expression to resolve the endpoint instead of looking for an endpoint command parameter.
member.remarks: The value is resolved via .

member.name: T:Naninovel.Metadata.BranchTraits
member.summary: Describes nature of branching caused by a execution; may have multiple flags specified via bitmask.

member.name: F:Naninovel.Metadata.BranchTraits.Endpoint
member.summary: Playback is navigated to a specific .
member.remarks: The endpoint is resolved either via or (when not specified) via a command parameter with context which specifies the endpoint to which the playback is redirected.

member.name: F:Naninovel.Metadata.BranchTraits.Nest
member.summary: Playback is navigated to the commands nested under the command.
member.remarks: Command is expected to have specified.

member.name: F:Naninovel.Metadata.BranchTraits.Interactive
member.summary: Branching depends on user interaction, eg picking a choice.

member.name: F:Naninovel.Metadata.BranchTraits.Return
member.summary: Playback flow, once branched, is expected, at some point, to return to the next command with the same nesting level.

member.name: F:Naninovel.Metadata.BranchTraits.Switch
member.summary: Denotes that the command is a switch root and starting a switch block, ie the playback may end up at one of the consequent commands which have a equal to the ID of this command and skip all the others in the same block.

member.summary: Attempts to resolve execution conditions for specified command in the context of specified script. Will check condition of the command itself, as well as nested host (eg, nested @if or @choice blocks) in case the command is nested.

Script lines. One of the lines is expected to contain specified command.

member.summary: Allows re-using object instances to limit heap allocations.

member.summary: Configures the pool behaviour.

member.summary: The initial size of the pool.

member.summary: The pool size limit, at which point it'll overflow and ignore returned objects, allowing them to be garbage-collected.

member.summary: Callback to invoke on the rented objects.

member.name: P:Naninovel.ObjectPool`1.Options.OnReturn
member.summary: Callback to invoke on the returned objects.

member.name: P:Naninovel.ObjectPool`1.Options.OnDrop
member.summary: Callback to invoke on the dropped objects: ignored on return due to overflow or objects dropped on .

member.summary: The total number of objects managed by the pool.

member.summary: Number of rented objects, ie rented and not returned.

member.name: P:Naninovel.ObjectPool`1.Available
member.summary: Number of "free" objects, ie rented and returned, available for rent w/o allocation.

member.name: T:Naninovel.Defer
member.summary: Invokes action specified in constructor on dispose.
member.remarks: Disposable structs won't box inside "using" context due to a .NET runtime optimization. Use when state is needed to prevent delegate allocation.

member.name: M:Naninovel.Defer.#ctor(System.Action)
member.summary: Invokes action specified in constructor on dispose.
member.remarks: Disposable structs won't box inside "using" context due to a .NET runtime optimization. Use when state is needed to prevent delegate allocation.

member.name: T:Naninovel.DeferNoop
member.summary: A mock disposable that does nothing on dispose. Use to prevent allocation when allocating actual disposable object is not necessary.
member.remarks: Disposable structs won't box inside "using" context due to a .NET runtime optimization.

The character used to escape separator.

member.summary: Allows splitting and joining text while escaping the separator.

member.param: The character to split and join with.
The character used to escape separator.

member.name: M:Naninovel.TextSplitter.Split(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.ICollection{System.String})
member.summary: Splits specified string into substrings (entries) using specified character as separator and adds the split entries to specified collection. When the separator character is escaped, will not split, but un-escape the character.
member.remarks: Empty entries are added as empty strings.
member.param: The string to split.
The collection to insert split entries.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute
member.summary: Suppresses reporting of a specific rule violation, allowing multiple suppressions on a single code artifact.
member.remarks: is different than in that it doesn't have a . So it is always preserved in the compiled assembly.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class, specifying the category of the tool and the identifier for an analysis rule.
member.param: The category for the attribute.
The identifier of the analysis rule the attribute applies to.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Category
member.summary: Gets the category identifying the classification of the attribute.
member.remarks: The property describes the tool or tool analysis category for which a message suppression attribute applies.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.CheckId
member.summary: Gets the identifier of the analysis tool rule to be suppressed.
member.remarks: Concatenated together, the and properties form a unique check identifier.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Scope
member.summary: Gets or sets the scope of the code that is relevant for the attribute.
member.remarks: The Scope property is an optional argument that specifies the metadata scope for which the attribute is relevant.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Target
member.summary: Gets or sets a fully qualified path that represents the target of the attribute.
member.remarks: The property is an optional argument identifying the analysis target of the attribute. An example value is "System.IO.Stream.ctor():System.Void". Because it is fully qualified, it can be long, particularly for targets such as parameters. The analysis tool user interface should be capable of automatically formatting the parameter.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.MessageId
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional argument expanding on exclusion criteria.
member.remarks: The property is an optional argument that specifies additional exclusion where the literal metadata target is not sufficiently precise. For example, the cannot be applied within a method, and it may be desirable to suppress a violation against a statement in the method that will give a rule violation, but not against all statements in the method.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Justification
member.summary: Gets or sets the justification for suppressing the code analysis message.
